self_audit.audit_method_notes: Audit performed against the source files at /Users/derrickteo/codings/altru/ (index.html, couple.html, donor.html, giving-aura.html, giving-weddings.html, wedding-venues.html, angbao-guide.html, css/styles.css, js/app.js). No live browser capture or WAVE/axe run; contrast ratios are computed from declared CSS values, not measured on rendered pixels.

modernness.rationale: Typography is solid (Playfair Display + Lato, properly preconnected at index.html:20-22; clamp-based fluid sizing in styles.css:43-46). Hero gradient (styles.css:603) and 囍 glyph watermark (styles.css:610-628) are a brand-confident choice. Drops to 6 because: (a) the layout is bottled into a 900px container (styles.css:52) so the site reads as a phone-first stretch on a 1440 viewport; (b) heavy emoji-as-iconography (🧧💑✨🏛️ in nav/cards/CTAs) leans 2021-era-Notion rather than 2025-state-of-the-art; (c) gradient overuse — every primary surface is gradient-on-gradient (styles.css:177, 196, 322, 388, 603, 666, 685, 906); (d) the 'Sarah's Story' carousel (index.html:107-465) is creative but the inline <style> + emoji-character approach feels homemade rather than Linear/Stripe-tier polish.

findings.issue: max-width: 900px makes the desktop hero feel cramped at 1440×900; reads as a mobile site upscaled.
findings.fix: Bump container to 1100-1200px; introduce a wider .container-lg variant for marketing pages.

findings.issue: Five separate hero gradients each declared inline (styles.css:603, 885, 906; wedding-venues.html:25; angbao-guide.html:24). Drift risk + heavy gradient overuse.
findings.fix: Consolidate into one --hero-gradient token; reuse via .hero variants. Reduces 'gradient soup' defect.

findings.issue: Two competing accent CTAs (gradient red AND gradient gold) in the same hero — index.html:87-92 puts btn-gold next to btn-outline next to a third btn-outline.
findings.fix: One primary CTA per hero. Demote 'I'm Getting Married' to a secondary text-link or a ghost button below the fold.

findings.issue: Emoji-as-character animation feels indie-game-jam, not premium-wedding-tech. The brand voice (Playfair Display, IPC certification) is at war with 😒-emoji-shake.
findings.fix: Replace Sarah character with custom illustrations (Lottie or static SVG portraits) or move the storytelling above-the-fold copy and drop the emoji theatrics.

attractiveness.rationale: Strong palette discipline — red (#C8102E) + gold (#C9973A) + warm neutrals + pink accent is well-constrained and culturally on-brief for SG Chinese wedding context (styles.css:2-21). Playfair Display display type + Lato body is a tasteful, bridal-magazine pairing. Hero compositions on index.html, page-hero, aura-hero all share a 囍 watermark motif (styles.css:611, 894, 942) which gives visual cohesion. Drops from a 9 because: (a) no custom photography anywhere — the site is 100% emoji + gradient + Playfair (no real wedding photos, no real charity beneficiary photos); (b) altru-logo.jpg is a 308KB JPG (2 Apr 2026) used at 2rem display — wasteful and likely soft on retina; (c) the 'Blessing Certificate' (donor.html:303-342) uses ✦ corner glyphs and gradient gold which leans kitsch rather than considered.

findings.issue: 308KB JPG at root, declared width=1280 height=1280 but rendered at 2rem in styles.css:86. 65× over-served; soft on retina once compressed by JPG.
findings.fix: Replace with SVG or a 64×64 PNG/WebP. Drops first-paint weight by ~300KB.

findings.issue: Zero photographic content across all 7 pages. 100% emoji-as-image. Wedding sites without photos read as wireframes-not-yet-skinned.
findings.fix: Commission/license 6-10 photos: real SG wedding (hotel ballroom), couple holding QR, charity beneficiary, IPC certificate. Hero, How-It-Works, Trust section all need anchoring imagery.

findings.issue: Blessing Certificate combines gold border + ✦ corners + 囍 watermark + IPC seal — five decorative elements competing. Reads as a generic award-printable, not a premium digital artifact.
findings.fix: Strip to two motifs (e.g. just gold border + Altru wordmark). Lean into typography rather than ornament.

findings.issue: Icon style is inconsistent — emoji icons (🧧💑✨) sit next to numbered red gradient circles (styles.css:663-677) sit next to gold IPC seals.
findings.fix: Pick one icon language across all marketing surfaces. Recommend custom line-icons in --red for marketing and reserve emoji for emotional moments only.

coherence.rationale: Hard-capped at 5 per methodology §10.1. The hero copy 'You're Going Anyway.' (index.html:81) is brand-statement-not-what-we-sell. A first-time buyer must scroll past the hero, past Sarah's 6-panel story (~24 seconds at 4s/panel auto-advance), past 'How It Works', past Tax Relief Banner, past Featured Charities to reach a clear pricing/fee disclosure (the 95/5 banner at index.html:728-738). Pricing-as-fee-structure is technically findable but the actual 'what does this cost the guest' question is the entry-level wedge of the donation amount — never stated upfront. Worse, the nav has SIX competing entry points for first-time visitors (Home, Ang Bao Guide, Venue Guide, Send a Gift, Giving Stories, I'm Getting Married — index.html:65-72), with no top-level navigation differentiating 'I am a couple' from 'I am a guest who scanned a QR' from 'I am evaluating legitimacy'. The hero CTA stack also confuses with three buttons (Send Altruistic Angbao + I'm Getting Married + Ang Bao Guide — index.html:86-94) competing for the same hero scroll.

findings.issue: Hero headline 'You're Going Anyway.' is a brand-statement, not a what-we-sell. A guest who lands here from a QR has no idea this is a charity-donation platform within 5 seconds.
findings.fix: Replace H1 with 'Wedding red packets that also fund a charity — in the couple's name.' OR keep current line as eyebrow and put what-we-sell as H1.

findings.issue: Three competing CTAs (Send Altruistic Angbao + I'm Getting Married + Ang Bao Guide) — methodology §5.3 defect: 'Three competing CTAs in the hero with no visual hierarchy. Drops by 2.'
findings.fix: One primary CTA (most-likely-intent: 'Send a Gift'). Secondary intents go in nav, not hero. Move 'Ang Bao Guide' to a 'Resources' section.

findings.issue: Six nav items (Home, Ang Bao Guide, Venue Guide, Send a Gift, Giving Stories, I'm Getting Married) with no role-based grouping. Couple flow + guest flow + content marketing + brand all share one row.
findings.fix: Split into role tabs: 'For Couples' / 'For Guests' / 'Resources' (drops to Ang Bao + Venue + Giving Stories) / 'Trust'. Or: persistent role-toggle in hero ('I'm a couple | I'm a guest').

findings.issue: 6-panel auto-advancing emoji carousel (4s × 6 = 24s) lives between hero and How-It-Works — buyer cannot scroll-skim past it without losing context. Also the tone (passive-aggressive humour about Sarah being 'forced' to donate) is a coherence risk for a tax-regulated charity platform.
findings.fix: Move carousel below How-It-Works, set to manual advance not auto, and trim to 3 panels. Re-tone caption copy to celebrate altruism rather than mock the obligated guest.

findings.issue: 'Send-a-gift' flow (donor.html step 1) jumps to amount input without ever disclosing the 95/5 split before payment. Guest only sees fee structure if they scrolled the index.html landing first.
findings.fix: Add a single fee-disclosure line on donor.html step 1: '95% goes to your chosen charity. 5% platform fee charged to the charity, never to you.' Match the language at index.html:733.

ui_clarity.rationale: Three-intent stopwatch test (estimated, source-only): see findability_seconds. Couple-onboarding intent and guest-FAQ intent both have first-click resolution from the nav, but 'Is this legit?' / 'What charity gets my money?' is buried at index.html:686 (Trust & Transparency section, ~6th scroll), and the actual fee comparison table is at index.html:742 (~8th scroll). Mobile menu (.menu-toggle, styles.css:108-131) is a working hamburger but pops underneath sticky header due to no explicit z-index on .nav-links.open (styles.css:146) — the parent .site-header z-index:100 carries it, but the menu items have no dedicated z-index, which historically (per memory feedback_mobile_menu_zindex.md) is exactly the bug pattern that renders mobile menu items invisible. UI clarity also drops because the header itself is six-link-crowded at desktop widths, increasing cognitive load on the first click.

findability_seconds.couple_get_started_path: index.html nav → 'I'm Getting Married' button (red CTA, top-right). 1 click. Lands on couple.html which presents login/signup. Note: the same intent is also served by the hero secondary CTA at index.html:88-90.
findability_seconds.guest_qr_scan_path: index.html nav → 'Send a Gift'. 1 click → donor.html step 1. But: a guest who literally just scanned a QR at the wedding table goes via giving-aura.html?couple=<id>, which leads to a different CTA path altogether ('🌸 Send an Altru Gift' → donor.html). Two diverging guest entry points create flow ambiguity. The QR-scan-from-table user is not the same as a 'browsing the public site' user, and the site doesn't differentiate.
findability_seconds.is_this_legit_path: Scroll past Hero (4s) → Sarah Story (~6s skim) → How It Works (~3s) → Tax Relief Banner (~2s, mentions IPC) → Featured Charities (skim) → finally 'Built on Trust & Transparency' at index.html:686. Or: click any 250% link → exits to IRAS, doesn't answer 'is Altru itself legit'. Note: there is no 'About' or 'Trust' link in the nav, which is the ordinary user's first-click destination for legitimacy questions.

findings.issue: Six items, no 'About' or 'Trust' or 'How it Works' link. Legitimacy-question users must scroll-hunt, can't first-click-resolve.
findings.fix: Add 'How It Works' and 'Trust' (or anchor to #trust-transparency) as nav items. Drop 'Home' (logo serves that purpose).

findings.issue: Mobile menu has no explicit z-index. If any below-fold element ever uses position:fixed or z-index >100 (the .auth-panel could under specific layouts), mobile menu items risk being rendered under content. Pre-emptively fragile per known bug pattern (feedback_mobile_menu_zindex.md).
findings.fix: Add z-index:199 explicitly to .nav-links.open in the mobile @media block.

findings.issue: Auto-advancing carousel keeps first-time visitors from scanning past hero. Click-to-advance is fine, but 4-second auto-advance steals reading control.
findings.fix: Disable auto-advance OR pause auto-advance once any carousel control is touched. (Current code only pauses on hover at index.html:594-596 — touch-screen users never trigger.)

findings.issue: 4-step indicator is a long scroll on mobile and the 'Continue → Choose Charity' button is the only forward affordance — no breadcrumb or back-from-step-3-without-data-loss path is documented in copy. Acceptable but mid-tier.
findings.fix: Add 'Save & continue later' or session persistence (already partial via localStorage) made visible to user.

accessibility.rationale: TOOL-RUN STATUS: blocked. WAVE/axe was not run because audit method is source-only (per audit_method_notes above). Score is conservative-from-static-analysis per methodology §5.5 paragraph on blocked tools. Contrast: --red #C8102E on white = 5.78:1 (passes AA). White on hero gradient (#3D0812 → #E5234A) = primary text ratio passes (>7:1 against the dark end), but rgba(255,255,255,0.55) eyebrow text at index.html:770 against #C8102E end of gradient = ~3.0:1 — fails AA for body text, borderline for large text. Same risk: rgba(255,255,255,0.6) at index.html:152 over the very dark Sarah Story background #0f0205 actually passes (~9:1) — OK there. Alt text: logo has alt='Altru' (good); emoji are inline text (screen readers will read them which can be either great or noisy); no for content imagery (because there is none). Focus states: buttons inherit default browser focus (no custom :focus-visible defined in styles.css). 200% zoom: hero uses clamp() typography which should reflow, but the Sarah stage is height:260px fixed with font-size:4.5rem face — likely overflows at 200% zoom on mobile.

findings.selector: rgba(255,255,255,0.55) eyebrow text @ index.html:770 (CTA section over red gradient)
findings.issue: Eyebrow 'You've read this far. You're definitely going.' at 0.78rem with 55% white over #C8102E end of gradient is ~3.0:1 — fails AA 4.5:1 for normal text.
findings.fix: Bump to rgba(255,255,255,0.85) or use solid #FBF5DC (gold-light) which gives ~7.1:1 against #C8102E.

findings.issue: No :focus-visible styles in styles.css. Keyboard users see only browser-default focus rings, which on a styled .btn-primary gradient are barely visible.
findings.fix: Add `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--gold); outline-offset: 2px; }` once globally.

findings.issue: Fixed height:260px with font-size:4.5rem .sarah-face will overflow at 200% zoom on mobile (WCAG 1.4.10 reflow risk). Auto-advancing animation also fails WCAG 2.2.2 'pause/stop/hide' for content > 5s — there is no visible pause control (only on-hover, which is touch-inaccessible).
findings.fix: Replace fixed height with min-height + overflow:visible. Add explicit pause button (▸/⏸) next to the next/prev arrows. Honour prefers-reduced-motion to disable auto-advance entirely.

findings.selector: Emoji as icons across nav, hero, and trust cards
findings.issue: Emoji are read aloud by screen readers verbatim ('Red gift envelope ang bao guide red gift envelope') which makes nav-by-screen-reader fatiguing. Emoji in CTAs ('🧧 Send Altruistic Angbao') compound the issue.
findings.fix: Wrap decorative emoji in 🧧. Keep label text intact for AT users.

findings.issue: Not run on this audit cycle. Methodology §5.5 caps scoring optimism when tools blocked.
findings.fix: Re-run audit with Playwright + axe-core or WAVE browser extension; the live URL is unauthenticated (https://altru.asia) so capture is straightforward.

mobile_pass.summary: Mobile-readiness is the site's single biggest QA risk because >70% of SG couples and ~90% of QR-scan-at-table guests will land via a mobile browser. The site IS responsive — every page declares <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0'> and styles.css collapses to a single-column .how-grid + stacked .tax-banner under 640px. But four specific mobile failures need fixing before launch.

issues.issue: Mobile menu has no explicit z-index. Pre-emptive fragility — exact pattern that has bitten elitez/xinceai sites before (memory feedback_mobile_menu_zindex.md). If any sticky element below header gains z-index >100, menu items vanish.
issues.fix: Add z-index:199 to .nav-links.open. One-line CSS fix.

issues.issue: Fixed height:260px with absolutely-positioned mood-blob (220×220), props at 2.8rem font, and a center character at 4.5rem font. On 375px-wide iPhone SE the speech bubble (white-space:nowrap, line 240) WILL overflow horizontally on longer Sarah lines like 'Table 19 let's go! 🔥' (panel 6).
issues.fix: Set max-width:90% on .speech-bubble and remove white-space:nowrap. Test on 320px viewport (smallest baseline).

issues.issue: Three buttons in hero. On 375px each button stacks full-width. .btn-lg has padding:1rem 2.2rem = ~14rem each = 70vh of hero scroll for buttons alone before any social proof.
issues.fix: Reduce to 2 hero CTAs on mobile (hide third behind a 'More options' affordance, or kill 'Ang Bao Guide' from hero entirely).

issues.issue: 4-step indicator with .steps display:flex + overflow-x:auto gives horizontal-scroll-stepper on mobile — works mechanically but the connector lines (.step-item:not(:last-child)::after at styles.css:430) use width:80% from left:60% which on a ~80px-wide step item is 64px line — fine, but at 320px viewport with text labels 'Gift Details' wraps and breaks the layout.
issues.fix: Truncate step labels on mobile (text-overflow:ellipsis) or shorten to 1-word labels: 'Gift', 'Charity', 'Info', 'Pay'.

issues.selector: Sarah Story carousel touch behaviour @ index.html:594-596
issues.issue: Auto-advance pauses only on `mouseenter` (line 595). Touchscreen users have no way to pause — they tap an arrow but the 4s timer keeps running and steals their next read.
issues.fix: Add `touchstart` listener that calls clearTimeout(autoTimer); restart on touchend after 8s OR remove auto-advance entirely on (pointer:coarse) media query.

issues.issue: On mobile the tax-banner stacks (flex-direction:column) but the giant 250% number is centered while the IPC Certified badge floats at the bottom — visual hierarchy inverts (number first, headline second on mobile only).
issues.fix: Reorder DOM so headline-then-badge-then-number reads consistently on both viewports, OR use CSS order: properties at 640px breakpoint.

mobile_pass.verdict: Mobile is functional but not polished. Two HIGH severity issues (menu z-index pre-emptive risk, Sarah carousel speech-bubble overflow) need fixing before any IG/TikTok/wedding-planner referral campaign goes live, because that traffic is 90% mobile.

intent_1_couple_get_started.intent: I'm a couple — how do I get started?
intent_1_couple_get_started.path: index.html → click red 'I'm Getting Married' button (top-right of nav, also visible as secondary hero CTA) → couple.html
intent_1_couple_get_started.friction_notes: First-click is good, but couple.html lands on a login screen (couple.html:79-107) before any 'create account / how it works for couples' onboarding. A fresh couple who has never used Altru hits a password prompt with no obvious 'sign up' affordance — the only visible escape is the back-arrow or the navigation. There is no public-facing 'For Couples' explainer page.

intent_2_guest_qr_scan.intent: I'm a guest — what's this red packet QR code on the table?
intent_2_guest_qr_scan.path: Most likely: QR → giving-aura.html?couple=<id> → guest reads couple's name + chosen charity → click '🌸 Send an Altru Gift' (giving-aura.html:286) → donor.html step 1. Alternative: QR → index.html → 'Send a Gift' nav → donor.html.
intent_2_guest_qr_scan.friction_notes: Path A (giving-aura) is the intended flow but requires the couple's QR to point at giving-aura.html?couple=<id>, not at index.html. If the couple inadvertently shares the index.html link, the guest must scroll-hunt for 'Send a Gift'. Larger issue: nowhere on the landing does it explain 'You scanned a QR at a wedding? Tap here →'. No QR-arrival affordance, no 'For Guests' tab. Guests at a noisy wedding banquet with weak hotel WiFi need ONE big primary button, not Sarah's 24-second story.

intent_3_legitimacy_charity.intent: Is this legit / IRAS-approved? What charity does my money go to?
intent_3_legitimacy_charity.clicks: 0 (scroll-only) but 6 viewports of scroll on desktop
intent_3_legitimacy_charity.path: index.html → scroll past hero (no trust signal) → past Sarah Story (no trust signal, ~24s if auto-advance not paused) → past How It Works (mentions IRAS link in passing) → past Tax Relief Banner (1st substantive trust mention: 'IPC Certified' badge) → past Featured Charities → past Trust & Transparency section at index.html:686 (the actual answer: PDPA, Charities Act, 95/5 fee, fund-raiser code) → past 95/5 banner with fee comparison.
intent_3_legitimacy_charity.friction_notes: TWO trust failures: (1) No 'About' or 'Trust' link in nav — legitimacy intent has no first-click answer. (2) The trust section is correctly populated (Gross Payment Rule, PDPA, Licensed Fund-raiser, Real-Time Transparency at index.html:692-723) but visually-styled as 4 small cards rather than a prominent 'Why Altru is safe' banner. (3) IPC charity list is at index.html:653-661 and lazy-renders from JS — no 'See all 13 charities' CTA from above the fold. (4) The IRAS '250% tax deduction' link works but exits to a long IRAS page that does not mention Altru — a guest hunting 'is this legit' opens IRAS, sees nothing about Altru, and bounces.

findings_table.issue: H1 ('You're Going Anyway.') is a tone-statement, not a what-we-sell. Three competing CTAs in hero. Caps Coherence at 5 by methodology §10.1.
findings_table.fix: Replace H1 with explicit description of service. Reduce hero CTAs to 1 primary + 1 secondary. Move Ang Bao Guide to nav.
findings_table.expected_impact: Coherence 5 → 7. Conversion lift on cold traffic; first-time guest understands the offer in <5s instead of 24s.

findings_table.issue: 6 nav items with no role-based grouping. No 'About', 'Trust', or 'How It Works' first-click destination. Couple/Guest/Browser intents share the same row.
findings_table.fix: Restructure to: For Couples · For Guests · How It Works · Trust · Resources (sub-menu: Ang Bao + Venue + Stories). Or persistent role-toggle in hero.
findings_table.expected_impact: UI Clarity 5 → 7. 'Is this legit?' intent drops from 14s to 4s. Couple onboarding clarity improves.

findings_table.issue: No explicit z-index on .nav-links.open. Pre-emptively fragile per known bug pattern; menu items can render invisible if any below-fold sticky/fixed element exceeds z-index 100.
findings_table.expected_impact: Eliminates a class of invisible-mobile-menu bug before it ships. Critical because >70% traffic is mobile.

findings_table.issue: 24-second auto-advancing emoji carousel between hero and How-It-Works. Tone (mocking obligated guest) is brand-risk for a regulated charity platform. Speech-bubble nowrap overflows on mobile. Auto-advance steals user reading control. WCAG 2.2.2 fail.
findings_table.fix: Move below How-It-Works. Trim to 3 panels. Disable auto-advance OR honour prefers-reduced-motion. Re-tone to celebrate altruism rather than mock obligation. Remove white-space:nowrap from .speech-bubble.
findings_table.expected_impact: Improves Coherence and Mobile and Accessibility simultaneously. Reduces hero-to-CTA scroll depth.

findings_table.issue: Donor flow (donor.html step 1) does not disclose 95/5 fee structure before payment. The fee narrative is on landing page only.
findings_table.fix: Add one-line fee disclosure on donor.html step 1: '95% goes to charity. 5% platform fee charged to charity, never deducted from your gift.' Mirror language from index.html:733.
findings_table.expected_impact: Trust signal at the moment of commitment. Reduces refund/dispute risk.

findings_table.issue: 'Built on Trust & Transparency' lives at the 6th scroll on desktop. No anchor or first-click path from nav. Fee comparison table buried 8th scroll.
findings_table.fix: Add 'Trust' nav item linking to #trust-transparency. Promote 95/5 banner to a sticky badge or a hero sub-element. Consider a 'Why Altru is safe' modal accessible from any page header.
findings_table.expected_impact: Legitimacy intent findability 14s → 3s. Reduces guest bounce on first arrival from QR.

findings_table.issue: max-width: 900px makes 1440-viewport landings feel cramped (450px of side margin). Site reads as a phone-first stretch rather than a 2025 desktop layout.
findings_table.fix: Bump container to 1100-1200px for marketing pages. Keep .container-sm (600px) for forms.
findings_table.expected_impact: Modernness 6 → 7. Desktop-first wedding-planner referrals (B2B partnership leads) get a more credible first impression.

findings_table.issue: Zero photographic content. 100% emoji + gradient. Wedding-tech buyer expects real hotel/couple/charity-beneficiary photos.
findings_table.fix: Commission 6-10 photos: SG hotel ballroom, couple holding QR card, charity beneficiary, IPC certificate. Place in hero + How-It-Works + Trust.
findings_table.expected_impact: Attractiveness 7 → 9. Major lift in IG/TikTok shareability of OG cards (currently the OG image is a generic Altru graphic per index.html:12).

findings_table.issue: No :focus-visible CSS in styles.css. Keyboard users see only browser-default focus rings, barely visible against gradient buttons.
findings_table.fix: Add `.btn:focus-visible, .nav-links a:focus-visible, .charity-option:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--gold); outline-offset: 2px; }` globally.
findings_table.expected_impact: Closes one WCAG 2.4.7 gap. Required for any IRAS/government partnership conversation.

findings_table.issue: Logo is 308KB JPG declared at 1280×1280 but rendered at 2rem. 65× over-served, slow on mobile 4G.
findings_table.fix: Replace with SVG (or 64×64 PNG/WebP). Drops first-paint by ~300KB.
findings_table.expected_impact: First Contentful Paint -0.3s on 3G. Better Core Web Vitals score, helps SEO.

findings_table.issue: First-time-couple lands on a password-only login with no visible 'sign up' or 'create your wedding page' affordance.
findings_table.fix: Add a clearly-styled 'New here? Create your wedding page →' CTA below the login button.
findings_table.expected_impact: Couple onboarding clarity. Eliminates 'where do I sign up' support tickets.

findings_table.issue: Screen readers verbalise emoji ('Red envelope I'm Getting Married'). Some emoji unmounted in aria-hidden, others not. Inconsistent.
findings_table.fix: Wrap all decorative emoji in <span aria-hidden='true'>. Keep emoji that ARE the message (e.g. ❤️ in a wedding context) without aria-hidden.

competitive_design_references.name: Zola
competitive_design_references.borrow: Role-based homepage routing — Zola's nav splits clearly into 'Wedding Planning' / 'Registry' / 'Invitations' / 'For Guests' as top-level tabs. Solves Altru's #2 finding (couple-vs-guest entry-point ambiguity).

competitive_design_references.name: The Knot
competitive_design_references.borrow: Hero photography — every Knot landing has a real couple photo + a single dominant CTA. Solves Altru's #8 finding (zero photographic content) and #1 finding (3-CTA hero).

competitive_design_references.name: Giving.sg
competitive_design_references.borrow: Trust-first layout — Giving.sg surfaces 'Verified by NCSS' / 'Tax-deductible' badges in a sticky band, not a 6th-scroll section. Also: the charity browse cards use real beneficiary photos. Solves Altru's #6 finding (trust positioning) and #8 (imagery).

competitive_design_references.name: Charity: Water
competitive_design_references.borrow: 100% goes to the field — model for a single, memorable fee/transparency message ('100% Model'). Altru's '95/5' framing is fine but currently buried mid-page — Charity: Water puts theirs above the hero fold and ties it to a counter (literal $ raised). Solves Altru's #5 finding (fee transparency) and supports the broader trust narrative.

design_debt_flags.top_competitor_id: n/a (no competitor audit available in same project run)
design_debt_flags.note: Self-audit only — Agent 1 has not produced competitors.json for Altru's category. Once direct competitors (e.g. Giving.sg wedding-modes, RAY of Hope, ToGather) are scored, re-flag Coherence gap. Hard cap of 5 is the binding constraint here.
Same — re-flag once competitor scores exist. 14-second legitimacy-intent findability is the single biggest UI Clarity defect.
WAVE/axe was not run in this audit cycle. Score is conservative-from-static-analysis. Re-run with a real browser tool before treating this score as final.

self_audit.notes: Coherence (5) and UI Clarity (5) are hard-capped per methodology — pricing/fee transparency in donor flow + legitimacy-intent buried at scroll-6 + 3-CTA hero. Mobile menu has no explicit z-index (pre-emptive bug). WAVE/axe blocked by source-only audit method; re-run before launch.
